title: Share your problem statement(s) with your table
bullets: My pain point at work is that I spend time (doing a specific task).
If I could reduce or change this by (specific improvement),
I would gain (time, quality, or clarity impact).

right.label: Goals
bullets: Narrow to one clear workflow (MVP)
Break work into steps
Define inputs, constraints, and stakeholders
Explicitly describe what success looks like and how it will be evaluated

rows.body: You are a [add an expert, such as a "social media expert"] and your objective is to [add goal, such as “create LinkedIn posts about events”]
“To complete these instructions, follow these steps”: Step 1, Step 2, Step 3 (etc)”
“When you are done, write your final response”: A summary paragraph of the work done; 3 bullet points, one per next step

caption: Use the + menu to add files, projects, and connectors — then turn on Research to investigate a question.

title: Build a Claude Project to answer user questions about material.
card: Start a new project and add some content you want people to know (e.g. your website's FAQ)
Write a set of instructions telling the Project to respond to user questions only using the materials you gave it.

title: Coach / Librarian
bullets: “I want an AI that answers my and my team's questions about our company materials.”
Prompt: Describe how it should interact with your team and your materials.
Files: Share the materials you want it to pull from.

title: Code(ish)!
bullets: Vanilla Claude Chat can write simple code for you!
Don't think about it as “coding” - you are merely asking for a computer to do something annoying for you.
The mad lib to start with: “Make me __________ that can run in my browser” / “Make me an HTML file that…”
It's very helpful to be very specific about the STEPS section of your prompt if your task is specific or complex.

title: Have Claude make you a new website
card: Ask Claude to “recreate the website at <URL>”
Optional: Give it any requests for style, features you want

title: Recreate a site from its URL
subtitle: Ask Claude to recreate a site at a URL — it reads the front-end design for best practices, fetches details, and rebuilds the page.
note: Original slide showed a live website-recreation screenshot of the source org's own site (off-brand for IAF). On reshoot, drop in a screenshot of an IAF or brand-neutral site being rebuilt.

title: Grab a piece of paper and draw the steps of how you make toast.
card: When you're finished, share your steps with your team.
Notice what you had the same/different.

caption: Drawing how you make toast — the classic systems-thinking warm-up.
A workflow is a chain of nodes. Each box is a node; the arrows are the flow between them.
The gap is usually not the tool — it is the missing handoff between "this looks useful" and "this runs inside the business every week."

title: For any single step in a workflow, you can break it down into:

steps.label: Trigger
steps.sub: The signal to start the step

steps.label: Actor
steps.sub: The person or thing that completes the step

steps.label: Inputs
steps.sub: Info or data needed to complete the step (this is optional!)

steps.label: Action
steps.sub: The actions the actor does to complete the step

steps.label: Success
steps.sub: The result of the step and how you know it's successful

eyebrow: TRY IT: ZOOM IN ON ONE STEP (NODE)
title: Apply the Steps to this Node: Getting Toast Out of the Toaster

steps.label: Trigger
steps.sub: You hear the toaster ding

steps.label: Actor
steps.sub: The person grabbing toast

steps.label: Inputs
steps.sub: None needed, just hunger! (Maybe some jam)

steps.label: Action
steps.sub: You reach out your hand and pick up the toast

steps.label: Success
steps.sub: The toast is out of the toaster!

caption: The five components of a node: Trigger, Actor, Inputs (optional), Action, Success.
note: Merged slides 22 (generic definitions) and 23 (toast example applied); 23 is the fullest applied version. Generic defs: Trigger = the signal to start the step; Actor = the person or thing that completes the step; Inputs = info or data needed to complete the step (optional); Action = the actions the actor does to complete the step; Success = the result of the step and how you know it's successful.

title: “I want AI to match our graduates to the best jobs.”
subtitle: A real ask we'll map into a workflow.
note: Merged slides 24 and 25 (identical; 25 is a struck-through animation build). Used src 25 = last slide of group.

title: Problem: How to Match Our Graduates to the Best Job

steps.label: Node 1
steps.sub: “I search five job sites for new jobs since my last search”

steps.label: Node 2
steps.sub: “I save the jobs that match my students’ interests

steps.label: Node 3
steps.sub: “I present the matches to each student. If they are interested we apply.

caption: A workflow is a chain of nodes, each handing off to the next.

eyebrow: TRY IT: ZOOM IN ON ONE STEP (10 MIN)
title: Map out the aspects of Node 1 in the job search workflow
card: “I search five job sites for new jobs since my last search”
Apply the five components: trigger, actor, inputs, action, success
question: What are the trigger, actor, inputs, action, and success for Node 1?

title: The node model maps to how you design your AI to run that step!

steps.label: Trigger
steps.sub: What starts your AI process (e.g. opening ChatGPT, a notification)

steps.label: Actor
steps.sub: The AI

steps.label: Inputs
steps.sub: What data/ context does it need? Role and goal

steps.label: Action
steps.sub: The steps you laid out in your prompt

steps.label: Success
steps.sub: The output and how we know it did a good enough job

title: The node model maps perfectly to how you design your AI to run that step!

left.label: Process Today
bullets: TRIGGER — You need more jobs
ACTOR — Human
INPUT — List of Sites
STEPS — Go to website; Input key words; Add job links to a list with a description
SUCCESS — List of jobs

right.label: Process with AI (your prompt sections)
bullets: TRIGGER — A calendar notification goes off
ACTOR — Claude Project
INPUT — Role, Goal, and Context
STEPS — Search the sites; Get new jobs (with context on what a good, new job is)
SUCCESS — Output: 5-10 jobs based on all the sites, the URLs

note: Merged slides 30 & 31 (near-identical node-anatomy table; 31 only relabels the 'Process with AI' column with prompt-section names Role/Goal/Context, Steps, Output). Combined the fullest content from both into one record at the last slide number (31).

title: Map your workflow
card: Choose one specific workflow you want to improve based on your Mad Lib
Map the workflow as it happens today (draw it out!):
Identify one step that feels repetitive, slow, or unclear. Break down trigger, actor, inputs, action, success
question: (45 min) We’ll check back in here at 12:00. If you finish early, you can try creating the prompt that would run this part of the workflow.

title: Fully human → Fully automated

steps.label: Fully Human
steps.sub: All nodes are performed manually by a human without any AI assistance.

steps.label: AI-Assisted Human
steps.sub: Human performs some nodes with support from AI tools that handle some of the more repetitive or time consuming nodes.

steps.label: Fully Automated
steps.sub: AI performs all or most of the nodes with minimal or no human intervention, handling tasks from start to finish.

caption: The spectrum of AI involvement across your workflow nodes.

rows.lead: You don’t have a clear process
rows.body: Make some decisions about how it could work just for today
Pick a smaller subsection of it

rows.lead: “Steps” are insufficient to capture all the info to do the activity
rows.body: Add a little “context” list of info you’d want to give someone

title: Finalize your workflow
card: 02  If you're ready, write this into a prompt and try it!
question: Try Toast Bot if you're stuck (or curious!) — https://bit.ly/toastbot

title: Share what you built
intro: Post in the cohort channel, or email the ImpactAI Foundry team.
bullets: In a few sentences, describe the workflow you worked on today
Snap a pic if you drew it!
(Optional) Make a note of 1-2 other problems you're considering working on, whether directly AI implementation or not, if you've got 'em.
note: TODO: set the real cohort channel + team email.

title: Reflections
bullets: Introduce yourself and your org
What workflow you worked on today
What went well? What was a challenge?
Any other reflections?

title: Take 5 minutes and reflect on today.
subtitle: Find this week's survey link in your cohort channel.
note: TODO: add IAF's own weekly-survey link.

title: What's next

rows.body: See you here in two weeks — [insert date and time]!
Chat on the cohort channel or reach out to the ImpactAI Foundry team.
